Title: Combined Transcriptomic and Metabolomic Analysis Reveals an Ethylene-Activated Regulatory Model on Monoterpenoid Indole Alkaloid Biosynthesis in Catharanthus roseus

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for transient CrEIN3-GFP overexpression in petals; native SGD promoter used in dual-LUC/Y1H/ChIP assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by CrEIN3 overexpression and downregulated by CrEIN3 or CrEIL1 silencing; ethylene responsive
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Gateway step from strictosidine toward downstream MIA branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes strictosidine to strictosidine aglycone, opening entry into downstream monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required gateway step for downstream vinblastine/anhydrovinblastine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer bisindole alkaloids and related defense metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of SGD is associated with enhanced downstream MIA flux and increased vinblastine/anhydrovinblastine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SGD was identified as a direct target of CrEIN3. 2. CrEIN3 bound the ATGTA-box in the SGD promoter by Y1H, EMSA and ChIP-qPCR. 3. Mutation of the ATGTA-box abolished CrEIN3-mediated promoter activation. 4. SGD is presented as a gateway enzyme propelling downstream MIA biosynthesis toward bisindole alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC promoter activation; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; gene expression profiling in CrEIN3 OE/VIGS lines
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key gateway/committed control step supported by direct transcriptional targeting; not explicitly proven rate-limiting enzymatically
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering or stacking with CrEIN3/CrEIL1 to enhance downstream MIA production in plants or heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE252019; metabolomics by UPLC-Q/TOF MS and NMR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper highlights SGD as the direct CrEIN3 target among multiple tested promoters; discussed as gateway enzyme in ET regulatory model.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in pHB/p2300 transient overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in flowers and old leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in ethylene-responsive module; associated with ETH-responsive green/pink network
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis, especially downstream/bisindole branch
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation and repression; direct promoter binding to SGD; indirect activation of additional pathway genes and activator TFs; repression of repressor TFs
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SGD; ORCA3; SAT; CS; TS; PRX1; CrERF5; GBF1; ZCT1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SGD directly and activates ORCA3, SAT, CS, TS, PRX1 and CrERF5; represses GBF1 and ZCT1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive MIAs including anticancer bisindole alkaloids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator required for ethylene-induced accumulation of catharanthine and anhydrovinblastine and promotes vinblastine, anhydrovinblastine, and ajmalicine accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ethylene-signaling mediator and decisive positive regulator of downstream MIA flux, particularly bisindole alkaloids
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrEIN3 was identified from ETH-responsive coexpression and GENIE3 networks as a key TF correlated with catharanthine and anhydrovinblastine. 2. Arabidopsis ein3 eil1 complementation confirmed conserved ethylene signaling function. 3. VIGS of CrEIN3 reduced ethylene-induced catharanthine and anhydrovinblastine accumulation. 4. Overexpression increased ORCA3/CrERF5 and multiple MIA enzyme genes while repressing GBF1/ZCT1. 5. CrEIN3 directly binds the SGD promoter ATGTA-box and activates it in vivo and in vitro.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/WGCNA/GENIE3; Arabidopsis mutant complementation; VIGS; transient overexpression; metabolite profiling by LC-MS; qRT-PCR; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for boosting vinblastine/anhydrovinblastine; can be stacked with SGD or CrEIL1/BIS2 for stronger MIA production in plant or yeast platforms
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE252019; metabolomics by UPLC-Q/TOF MS and NMR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrEIN3 appears to act mainly on downstream MIA pathway through direct SGD activation and broader transcriptional reprogramming.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in pHB/p2300 transient overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in young leaves and flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with turquoise ETH/JA-responsive network; strong regulatory relationship with iridoid and vindoline genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis, especially upstream iridoid pathway and downstream bisindole output
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional activation through protein-protein interaction; synergistic coactivation with BIS2; repression of repressor genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BIS2-associated targets including 8-HGO; 7-DLGT; 7-DLH; LAMT; ORCA3; also DXS1; SLS1; TDC; STR; SGD; REDOX2; SAT; DAT; CrWRKY1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ORCA3, BIS2, CrWRKY1, CrERF5 and multiple iridoid/indole/MIA genes; represses GBF1 and ZCT1; synergistically enhances BIS2 activation of iridoid promoters
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ethylene/ethephon; integration with jasmonate-responsive BIS2 module
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive MIAs including anticancer vinblastine and defense-related alkaloids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that promotes secologanin and downstream MIA accumulation; required for full ethylene-induced MIA production and synergizes with BIS2 to enhance bisindole alkaloids
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ethylene-signaling regulator controlling upstream iridoid flux and cooperating with BIS2 to strongly enhance vinblastine pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrEIL1 was identified by network analysis as a key TF linked to iridoid/vindoline genes. 2. Arabidopsis ein3 eil1 complementation showed conserved ethylene signaling function. 3. VIGS reduced ETH-induced catharanthine and anhydrovinblastine and decreased many pathway transcripts. 4. Overexpression increased ORCA3, BIS2, CrWRKY1, CrERF5 and multiple MIA genes and elevated secologanin, ajmalicine and vinblastine. 5. CrEIL1 does not directly bind tested promoters but physically interacts with BIS2 by Y2H, BiFC, pull-down and Co-IP. 6. Co-overexpression with BIS2 synergistically elevated iridoid genes and secologanin, vindoline, ajmalicine, anhydrovinblastine and vinblastine.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/WGCNA/GENIE3; Arabidopsis mutant complementation; VIGS; transient overexpression; LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; dual-LUC; Y1H negative evidence; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; Co-IP; co-overexpression assays; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent gene-stacking candidate with BIS2 for enhancing iridoid precursor supply and MIA production in plants or microbial platforms
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE252019; metabolomics by UPLC-Q/TOF MS and NMR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts differently from CrEIN3: no direct promoter binding detected for tested genes; functions via interaction with JA-induced BIS2.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient overexpression/co-overexpression assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: JA-responsive according to discussion/background and coexpression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly correlated with CrEIL1 in coexpression network
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Iridoid pathway branch feeding MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; synergistic coactivation with CrEIL1 via physical interaction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates iridoid pathway promoters and, with CrEIL1, enhances activation of 8-HGO, 7-DLGT, 7-DLH, LAMT and ORCA3
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate; ethylene integration through CrEIL1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors and bioactive MIAs including anticancer alkaloids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-overexpression with CrEIL1 enhances iridoid gene expression and increases secologanin and downstream MIAs beyond single-gene overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent decisive co-regulator of precursor supply to bisindole MIA production when partnered with CrEIL1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS2 was identified as the specific CrEIL1-interacting TF. 2. BIS2 alone activated 8-HGO, 7-DLGT and 7-DLH promoters. 3. Physical interaction with CrEIL1 was supported by Y2H, BiFC, pull-down and Co-IP. 4. Co-expression with CrEIL1 synergistically activated iridoid pathway genes and increased secologanin, vindoline, ajmalicine, anhydrovinblastine and vinblastine.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; Co-IP; dual-LUC; transient co-overexpression with metabolite profiling and qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking partner with CrEIL1 for maximizing precursor supply and end-product alkaloid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Jasmonate-related regulation integrated with ethylene response
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE252019; metabolomics by UPLC-Q/TOF MS and NMR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although previously known, BIS2 is experimentally central in this paper because its interaction with CrEIL1 was functionally validated and affects metabolite output.
